title: Carjacking suspect shot and killed in Roxbury, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston police shot and killed a carjacking suspect in Roxbury after he accelerated a stolen vehicle towards them.
The incident occurred near 10 Linwood Square at around 9:45 p.m. following reports of a carjacking.
The suspect, who remains unidentified, was taken to a hospital where he was pronounced dead; officers are in good condition.

title: Colin Dorgan, who lost 3 family members in Pawtucket shooting, scores game-winning goal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Colin Dorgan scored the game-winning goal for Blackstone Valley Co-op in a 3-2 win over Portsmouth.
Dorgan lost three family members in a February shooting at Dennis M. Lynch Arena in Pawtucket.
The Blackstone Valley team wears hearts on jerseys to honor Dorgan's mother, brother, and grandfather.

title: ‘We are in the midst of winter versus spring here’: Ski resorts are closing operations, reducing lift prices, limiting lifts
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Ski resorts in Massachusetts and Vermont closed operations due to heavy rain and wind on Thursday.
Wildcat Mountain in New Hampshire reduced prices and limited lift operations due to weather conditions.
Mount Snow and Jay Peak in Vermont adjusted lift operations due to high winds and wet snow conditions.

title: Two officers rescue woman found asleep amid house fire in Merrimac
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two officers rescued a woman found asleep during a house fire in Merrimac early Wednesday morning.
Resident Jeff Strogney saw 10 to 15-foot flames and called 911 after failed attempts to alert occupants.
Officers Paul DeCoste and Adam White entered and evacuated the woman safely, confirming no others were inside.

title: A 22-year-old man died in a three-car crash in Canton
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 22-year-old Marshfield man, Mathew McTiernan, died in a three-car crash on I-95 in Canton.
The crash occurred at 6:20 a.m., affecting two of the three lanes of traffic on I-95.
Massachusetts State Police from the Milton Barracks are investigating the cause of the crash.

title: Baby boomers feeling crushed by housing crunch
author: Lindsay Crudele
bullets: In 2025, baby boomers were 53% of home sellers and 42% of buyers, according to the National Association of Realtors.
Southbridge's Lebanon Hill Housing complex faced a rent increase up to 70%, causing financial strain for fixed-income residents.
Massachusetts seniors, now 23% of the population, face housing instability and lack of affordable housing options as they age.

title: You can now pass through TSA PreCheck at Logan using only your face. Should that make us nervous?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Logan Airport introduces TSA PreCheck Touchless ID, using facial recognition to streamline security for flyers.
The voluntary Touchless ID system allows travelers to verify identities through facial comparison without physical IDs.
Privacy advocates express concern over potential misuse of biometric data and lack of legal protections.

title: Kraft Group, Foxborough announce World Cup funding deal for Gillette Stadium security
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Kraft Sports & Entertainment Group and Boston Soccer 2026 agreed to fund security for the 2026 World Cup at Gillette Stadium.
Foxborough's costs for hosting the World Cup will be fully covered, with Boston Soccer 2026 providing advanced funding.
Seven World Cup matches are scheduled at Gillette Stadium, starting with Haiti vs. Scotland on June 13, 2026.

title: Natick mom to run the 2026 Boston Marathon for kinder communities
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Danica Champion, a 38-year-old ESL teacher from Natick, is running her first marathon in the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Champion is raising funds for Spark Kindness, a Natick organization promoting positive community values and youth empowerment.
The funds will support programs addressing issues like bullying and racism, aimed at fostering welcoming and supportive environments.

title: 10 predictions for the Red Sox this season
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet is predicted to win the Cy Young Award after finishing second last year with an 18-5 record.
Trevor Story aims to improve fielding after committing 19 errors last year, the third most among MLB shortstops.
Red Sox predicted to finish the season 92-70, winning the wild-card series but losing in the American League Division series.
